title: Dryopteris tenerifrons
taxonomy: Dryopteris tenerifrons is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. It belongs to the genus Dryopteris, which includes about 200 species of ferns. The family Dryopteridaceae is a part of the larger division Pteridophyta, which encompasses all ferns. This classification places Dryopteris tenerifrons within the kingdom Plantae. The specific classification is: Dryopteris tenerifrons (Maxon) C.Chr., Kingdom: Plantae, Division: Pteridophyta, Class: Polypodiopsida, Family: Dryopteridaceae, Genus: Dryopteris.
uses: Dryopteris tenerifrons, a species of fern, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. intro: Dryopteris tenerifrons, also known as the fern, is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including the reduction of inflammation, improvement of respiratory issues, and antioxidant properties that may aid in the prevention of cell damage.
